name: Monstera Deliciosa
description: Monstera Deliciosa is a stunning tropical plant known for its iconic split leaves. Native to the rainforests of Central America, it thrives in indirect bright light and brings a lush, exotic feel to any room.

caretaker.name: Sara Khan
caretaker.note: Passionate about tropical houseplants. Waters every 7 days.

careInstructions.label: Watering
careInstructions.detail: Every 7-10 days. Let top 2 inches of soil dry out between waterings.

careInstructions.label: Light
careInstructions.detail: Bright indirect light. Avoid direct harsh sunlight to prevent leaf burn.

careInstructions.label: Temperature
careInstructions.detail: 65–85°F (18–30°C). Keep away from cold drafts and AC vents.

careInstructions.label: Humidity
careInstructions.detail: Prefers 60%+ humidity. Mist leaves weekly or use a pebble tray.

careInstructions.label: Soil
careInstructions.detail: Well-draining potting mix with perlite. Repot every 1-2 years.

careInstructions.label: Pruning
careInstructions.detail: Remove yellow or damaged leaves at the base to encourage growth.

timeline.detail: Brought home as a 2-leaf cutting, 8 inches tall.
First fenestrated leaf appeared — a sign of happy growth!
Roots were circling the pot. Moved to a larger terracotta pot.
Noticed webbing on leaves. Treated with neem oil spray x3.
Shifted near east window for better morning light.
Plant transferred to Sara Khan after previous owner relocated.
Plant is now 3 feet tall with 12 beautiful leaves!

name: Snake Plant
description: Sansevieria Trifasciata, commonly known as the Snake Plant or Mother-in-Law's Tongue, is one of the most resilient houseplants. It thrives on neglect and is NASA-certified as an excellent air purifier.

caretaker.name: Ahmed Malik
caretaker.note: Loves low-maintenance plants. Waters sparingly, once every 2–3 weeks.

careInstructions.label: Watering
careInstructions.detail: Every 2-3 weeks in summer, monthly in winter. Overwatering is the main killer.

careInstructions.label: Light
careInstructions.detail: Tolerates low light but thrives in indirect bright light. Very adaptable.

careInstructions.label: Temperature
careInstructions.detail: 60–80°F (15–27°C). Sensitive to frost; never let it freeze.

careInstructions.label: Humidity
careInstructions.detail: Tolerates dry air well. No special humidity requirements.

careInstructions.label: Soil
careInstructions.detail: Use sandy, well-draining cactus soil. Drainage holes are essential.

careInstructions.label: Pruning
careInstructions.detail: Trim brown leaf tips with clean scissors. Remove dead leaves from base.

timeline.detail: Bought as a mature plant, already 2 feet tall.
Took 2 weeks to adjust to the low-light corner.
A baby pup appeared at the base — propagation potential!
Separated the pup into its own 4-inch pot.
Reduced watering to once a month. Plant is resting.

name: Fiddle Leaf Fig
description: Ficus Lyrata, the Fiddle Leaf Fig, is the statement plant of modern interiors. With its large, lush, violin-shaped leaves, it's Instagram-famous — but demands consistent care and the perfect spot.

caretaker.name: Zara Ahmed
caretaker.note: Interior design enthusiast. Dedicated to keeping this diva happy!

careInstructions.label: Watering
careInstructions.detail: Every 7 days when top inch of soil is dry. Consistency is key — no dramatic changes.

careInstructions.label: Light
careInstructions.detail: Needs bright, consistent indirect light. Do NOT move it — it hates change.

careInstructions.label: Temperature
careInstructions.detail: 60–80°F (15–27°C). Keep away from vents, doors, and cold windows.

careInstructions.label: Humidity
careInstructions.detail: 50–60% humidity. Use a humidifier in dry seasons.

careInstructions.label: Soil
careInstructions.detail: Rich, well-draining loamy soil. Fertilize monthly in spring/summer.

careInstructions.label: Pruning
careInstructions.detail: Prune in spring to control height. Wipe leaves with damp cloth monthly.

timeline.detail: Arrived as a 4-foot tree in a woven basket.
Dropped 3 leaves after being moved. Normal acclimation stress.
Settled near south window. No more moving!
5 new bright green leaves appeared at the top.
Began monthly liquid fertilizer routine.
Stopped fertilizing. Reduced watering frequency.

name: Golden Pothos
description: Epipremnum Aureum, or Golden Pothos, is the perfect starter plant. Trail it from a shelf, hang it in a basket, or let it climb a moss pole — it adapts to almost any condition and forgives beginner mistakes.

caretaker.name: Hassan Noor
caretaker.note: First-time plant parent. Learning as he goes — pothos is very forgiving!

careInstructions.label: Watering
careInstructions.detail: Every 7-10 days. Allow soil to dry completely between waterings.

careInstructions.label: Light
careInstructions.detail: Tolerates low light but golden variegation fades. Bright indirect light is ideal.

careInstructions.label: Temperature
careInstructions.detail: 65–85°F (18–30°C). Very adaptable — one of the toughest houseplants.

careInstructions.label: Humidity
careInstructions.detail: Not picky. Thrives in normal household humidity (40–60%).

careInstructions.label: Soil
careInstructions.detail: Regular potting mix works fine. Can even grow in just water!

careInstructions.label: Pruning
careInstructions.detail: Trim long vines to encourage bushiness. Propagate cuttings in water easily.

timeline.detail: Received as a water-propagated cutting — already rooted!
Transitioned from water to a 6-inch pot with potting mix.
Rapid trailing growth — placed on kitchen shelf to cascade.
Created 3 new baby plants to share with neighbors.
Incredible growth! A true kitchen centerpiece now.

careInstructions.label: Watering
careInstructions.detail: Check top 2 inches of soil before watering.

careInstructions.label: Light
careInstructions.detail: Bright indirect light is best for most indoor plants.

timeline.detail: Plant profile created in the system.
